scenario.context: Station 01: customer project kickoff with several follow-up orders through acceptance preparation.
scenario.mission: You work through the kickoff order and several follow-up orders along the project path with increasing responsibility.

station.profile: Project kickoff, objective, planning, implementation, testing, and handover
constraints: Keep risks and residual items transparent

customerRequest.subject: Station 01: Kickoff order for service portal customer module
customerRequest.body:
Please start a customer project for a prioritization module in the service portal.

After the kickoff order, additional project requests will follow covering objective changes, economic efficiency, project execution, and acceptance preparation.

questions.title: Structure the project kickoff
questions.prompt: Which first step makes the most sense when starting a customer project?

options.text: Start implementing immediately without clarifying roles and objectives
Define project roles, communication paths, and the target picture together
Mention only the final deadline and skip the rest

feedback.correct: Correct: Clarity on roles and objectives is the clean project kickoff.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Without kickoff clarity, the project becomes unstable.

questions.title: Objectives of the customer order
questions.prompt: Which contents belong in a robust objective definition? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Measurable target criteria and clear scope boundaries
Align acceptance criteria with the customer
Take over open requirements without checking them
Turn unclear points into active follow-up questions
Implement objective changes without documentation

feedback.correct: Correct: The objective definition is professionally sound.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please secure measurability and acceptance reference more strongly.

questions.title: Turn planning into implementation
questions.prompt: Put the steps from the target picture to robust project planning in order.
items: Confirm the project goal and delivery scope
correctOrder: Confirm the project goal and delivery scope

feedback.correct: Correct: This is a cleanly structured project plan.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please plan from the goal via effort and risk toward documentation.

questions.title: Calculate the kickoff package
questions.prompt: A kickoff package includes 16 hours of analysis at 68 EUR per hour. What is the cost?

feedback.correct: Calculated correctly.
feedback.wrong: Please multiply hours and rate correctly.

questions.title: Kickoff briefing for the customer
questions.prompt: Write a kickoff briefing (4-6 sentences) with target picture, rough plan, and one residual risk.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear target picture, viable planning approach, and a named residual risk.

feedback.correct: Correct: The briefing is clear and ready for decision.
feedback.wrong: Please separate the target picture, plan, and risk more clearly.

questions.title: Process an objective change professionally
questions.prompt: Which steps make sense when the objective changes? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Document the change and its impact on scope
Sharpen the acceptance criteria together
Push the change into production directly without consultation
Realign responsibilities within the project team
Keep the old planning running unchanged

feedback.correct: Correct: The change is processed in a controlled way.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please secure the links to scope, acceptance, and team more strongly.

questions.title: Adjust project organization
questions.prompt: Which reaction fits best when a new VIP feature is added?

options.text: Ignore the new feature so the plan is not put at risk
Extend roles and responsibilities clearly for monitoring and business alignment
options.rationale: Brings the changed objective into the organization.

options.text: Inform only the UI team and change nothing else

feedback.correct: Correct: Organization and responsibility have to grow with the change.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: The changed objective needs an adapted organizational logic.

questions.title: Calculate the scope surcharge
questions.prompt: The objective change requires 9 extra hours at 72 EUR per hour. What is the additional effort?

feedback.correct: Calculated correctly.
feedback.wrong: Please multiply extra hours and rate correctly.

questions.title: Change process
questions.prompt: Put the flow for a changed customer requirement in order.
items: Analyze the impact on time, cost, and risk
correctOrder: Analyze the impact on time, cost, and risk

feedback.correct: Correct: This is a clean change flow.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please place analysis and alignment before approval.

questions.title: Follow-up order 1 - Budget cap and economic efficiency
questions.subject: Cost limit per sprint and prioritization
questions.body: A cost limit applies to the next sprint. Please prioritize work packages, assess their benefit, and present the budget impact transparently.

questions.title: Economic efficiency under a budget cap
questions.prompt: What is the best professional response to the budget cap?

options.text: Cut scope without review
Prioritize work packages, review their benefit, and make the cost impact transparent
Ignore the budget requirement

feedback.correct: Correct: Prioritization and transparency are central here.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Budget steering needs clear prioritization and visibility of impact.

questions.title: Weigh cost and benefit cleanly
questions.prompt: Which points belong in a robust economic efficiency assessment? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Name the core benefit of each work package
Distinguish one-time costs from follow-up costs
Hide risks to approve faster
Compare alternatives with advantages and disadvantages
Choose only the lowest price without linking it to the objective
options.rationale: Can endanger achievement of the goal.

feedback.correct: Correct: This makes the economic assessment professionally sound.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please assess benefit, cost structure, and risks together.

questions.title: Add up sprint costs
questions.prompt: Three work packages cost 2150 EUR, 1980 EUR, and 2410 EUR. What is the total cost?

feedback.correct: Calculated correctly.
feedback.wrong: Please add all work packages correctly.

questions.title: Justify the budget adjustment
questions.prompt: Write a short note (4-6 sentences) explaining how the plan is adjusted under the budget cap.
questions.expectedOutline: Prioritization, benefit reference, and budget consequence in one clear decision.

feedback.correct: Correct: The note is economically and professionally traceable.
feedback.wrong: Please describe prioritization, benefit, and budget impact more concretely.

questions.title: Plan adjustment under a cost limit
questions.prompt: Put the flow for a plan adjustment under a cost limit in order.
items: Record the budget cap and must-have objectives
Calculate the impact on cost and risk
correctOrder: Record the budget cap and must-have objectives
Calculate the impact on cost and risk

feedback.correct: Correct: The flow is economically and logically clean.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please go through must-have objectives first, then prioritization and alignment.

questions.title: Follow-up order 2 - API risk during execution
questions.subject: Unstable API endangers core function
questions.body: An external API behaves unstably. Please control the risk and delivery capability in a disciplined way and document the additional effort.

questions.title: Risk control during execution
questions.prompt: Which measures help with an API blocker? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Document the blocker with priority and impact
Plan a fallback strategy for critical functions
Ignore the problem until shortly before delivery
Reflect status and decision points regularly in the team
Implement changes without documentation

feedback.correct: Correct: The risk is treated actively and in a steerable way.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please secure blocker transparency and fallback strategy more strongly.

questions.title: Secure the deliverable despite disruption
questions.prompt: With an unstable API, which decision is the most robust?

options.text: Stop delivery without informing the customer
Deliver a partial function with clear residual-item documentation and schedule the follow-up
options.rationale: A sustainable interim delivery with risk disclosure.

options.text: Talk up the result and hide the problem

feedback.correct: Correct: This keeps the project steerable and transparent.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Execution needs a clear residual-item and communication logic.

questions.title: Calculate buffer time
questions.prompt: 42 hours were planned, but 47 hours will actually be needed. How large is the additional effort?

feedback.correct: Calculated correctly.
feedback.wrong: Please calculate the difference between actual and planned effort correctly.

questions.title: Status communication for blockers
questions.prompt: Put the flow for robust blocker communication in order.

feedback.correct: Correct: This is a clear communication chain.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please present the situation first, then options and the decision.

questions.title: Follow-up order 3 - Status communication before acceptance
questions.subject: Align blocker status and acceptance step
questions.body: Please provide a robust status communication with a clear decision proposal and prioritized next steps.

questions.title: Blocker status update
questions.prompt: Write a short status update (4-6 sentences) with problem, impact, and next action.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear blocker status with impact and an actionable next step.

feedback.correct: Correct: The update is usable for team and customer.
feedback.wrong: Please structure the problem, impact, and next action more explicitly.

questions.title: Prioritize test evidence
questions.prompt: What is most important for acceptance preparation?

options.text: Show only the demo and skip the test protocols
Derive test cases from acceptance criteria and document the results
List only code changes

feedback.correct: Correct: This is exactly the evidence needed for acceptance.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Without test reference, approval remains uncertain.

questions.title: Complete the acceptance documents
questions.prompt: Which documents should be ready for acceptance? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Test protocol with open items
options.rationale: Shows maturity level and remaining work.

options.text: Short documentation for use and operations
Uncommented raw notes without structure
Presentation guide with core benefit and limits
options.rationale: Helps present the solution appropriately for the audience.

options.text: Open items without due date and owner

feedback.correct: Correct: This forms a complete acceptance basis.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please secure the test, documentation, and residual-item logic consistently.

questions.title: Follow-up order 4 - Test evidence and close
questions.subject: Acceptance window tomorrow 09:00
questions.body: Please finalize the test evidence, residual-item documentation, and closing note for tomorrow's acceptance.

questions.title: Acceptance path through reflection
questions.prompt: Put the flow from testing to the first project reflection in order.
items: Check acceptance criteria against test cases
Document test results and residual items
Present the solution with its core benefit
correctOrder: Check acceptance criteria against test cases
Document test results and residual items
Present the solution with its core benefit

feedback.correct: Correct: This is a consistent close-out chain.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please check and document first, then present and reflect.

questions.title: Assess test coverage
questions.prompt: Out of 28 planned acceptance tests, 25 were completed successfully. What is the success rate in percent (rounded)?

feedback.correct: Calculated correctly.
feedback.wrong: Please calculate the share of successful tests correctly.

questions.title: Acceptance and reflection note
questions.prompt: Write a closing note (5-7 sentences) with goal achievement, open item, risk, and next step.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear goal status, open items with risk, and a binding next step.

feedback.correct: Correct: The note is immediately usable for acceptance and follow-up work.
feedback.wrong: Please work out goal achievement, open items, and the next step more clearly.
